title: New and simplified method for drug combination studies by checkerboard assay
author: Bellio, Fagnani, et al.

steps.title: Procedure
steps.instruction: Day 1

steps.title: Preparation of culture media
steps.instruction: 1.Weigh an appropriate amount of powdered culture medium.2.Using a magnetic stirrer, dissolve the powder into deionised water until the required volume is reached (See recipe reported on the package insert or in the datasheet) and then sterilise by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 min.

steps.title: Preparation of culture media
steps.instruction: The culture media required are:•10 mL of MHB.•50–100 mL of 2 × CAMHB.

steps.title: Preparation of 100 mL of sterile saline solution (NaCl 0,9%)
steps.instruction: 1.Using a magnetic stirrer, dissolve 0.9 g of NaCl into 100 mL of deionised water and then sterilise by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 min.

steps.title: Preparation of antimicrobial or compound stock from powder
steps.instruction: 1.Weigh an appropriate amount of powder of drug A and drug B and dissolve each one into its appropriate solvent as requested by CLSI in the chapter: “Solvents and Diluents for Preparing Stock Solutions of Antimicrobial Agents” [3] or as recommended by the EUCAST guidelines [4]. If the antibiotic or compound is not listed in the guidelines, use the solvent that keeps the compound more stable (This information is often available in the datasheet).

steps.title: Preparation of microbial inoculum
steps.instruction: 1.Withdraw with a sterile loop an isolated colony from a nutrition agar plate.2.Inoculated the colony into a 10 mL of MHB.3.Grow the bacteria at 35±2°C in an orbital shaker incubator at 200 rpm under aerobic conditions for 18 h ±2.

steps.title: Preparation of microbial inoculum
steps.instruction: Note: After 18 h ±2 of incubation, under the condition indicated, the culture will reach the concentration of ∼ 109 CFU/mL.

steps.title: Preparation of microbial inoculum
steps.instruction: Day 2

steps.title: Preparation of plate for the two-drugs interaction measurement by checkerboard assay
steps.instruction: 1.Place 100 μL of the 2 × CAMHB medium in a V-shaped reservoir or sterile Petri dish and dispense into each well of the microplate, by multichannel pipette, proceeding by column from left to right or vice versa.

steps.title: Preparation and dispensing of drug A
steps.instruction: 1.Prepare 1.5 mL of stock solution (SA) in 2 × CAMHB medium to obtain a solution 4-fold more concentrated than the highest concentration of the drug A to be tested.2.Prepare 500 μL of 2 × stock solution (2 × SA) in 2 × CAMHB medium to obtain a solution 8-fold more concentrated than the highest concentration of the drug A to be tested.3.Dispense 100 μL of SA into row A from columns 1–11. (Fig. 1)Fig. 1Disposition of drug A in the 96-well microplate.Fig 14.Dispense 100 μL of 2 × SA into well A12. (Fig. 1)

steps.title: Preparation and dispensing of drug A
steps.instruction: Note: The purpose of adding the Stock solution 2 × SA is to keep constant the concentration of drug A during the next serial dilution (point 9).

steps.title: Preparation and dispensing of drug B
steps.instruction: 1.Prepare 1.0 mL of stock solution (SB) in 2 × CAMHB medium to obtain a solution 4-fold more concentrated than the highest concentration of drug B to be tested.2.Dispense 100 μL of SB into each column 12 wells. (Fig. 3).Fig. 3Disposition of drug B in the 96-well microplate and dilution by multichannel pipette.Fig 3

steps.title: Plate Incubation
steps.instruction: 1.Place the microplate with its cover into a static incubator at 35±2°C for 18±2 h.

steps.title: Plate Incubation
steps.instruction: Note: If there should be an excessive variation in volume during incubation, it is possible to seal the microplate by sealing film. Also, a tray with water can be added to the incubator to prevent the dry environment favouring evaporation from the plate.

steps.title: Plate Incubation
steps.instruction: Day 3

steps.title: Optical analysis by a microplate reader
steps.instruction: 1.Mix the contents of the wells using a multichannel pipette.2.Place the microplate in a microplate reader.3.Read the optical density (OD) at 600 nm or around 600 nm (e.g. 595 or 605).

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: From the optical density, it is possible to calculate the percentage of growth for each well.

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: Based on the distribution of the reagents that have been made, the plate is organised as follows (Fig. 4):■In column 1 from A to G wells, there are the double serial dilutions of drug A. A1 is the well where the drug A has the highest concentration (HA).■In the raw H from columns 12–2, there are the double serial dilutions of drug B. H12 is the well where the drug B has the highest concentration (HB).■The well H1 represent the bacterial growth control without drugs (100% growth).■All the other wells of the microplate represent all the 77 possible combinations formed by the combination of the serial dilutions of each antibiotic.Fig. 4The final setting of 96-well microplate.Fig 4

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: The percentage of growth in each well was calculated as:ODdrugcombinationwell−ODbackgroundODdrugfreewell−ODbackground×100

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: Note: ODbackground is the value of OD where no growth is appreciable or a value previously determined using the same method, where the reagents without bacteria were placed.

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: From the data, it is also possible to calculate the Minimal Inhibitory Concentration (MIC) for each combination. MIC is defined as the lowest drug concentration, which reduces bacterial growth by more than 80%.
